config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent Indonesia vs China native-or-English block. Each persona speaks only English or its own native language.
config.cell_description: One-side-native cross cell: Indonesia persona speaks Indonesian; China persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: One-side-native cross cell: Indonesia persona speaks Indonesian; China persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: DISAGREE. Menurut saya, kepentingan masyarakat itu penting, tetapi tidak boleh otomatis mengalahkan hak individu karena hak individu justru menjadi batas agar mayoritas tidak sewenang-wenang. Dalam pengalaman saya melihat masyarakat yang terlalu menekan individu, hasilnya sering bukan ketertiban yang sehat, melainkan ketakutan, ketidakadilan, dan orang-orang yang tidak berani bersuara. Kepentingan umum seharusnya dicapai dengan menghormati hak dasar setiap orang, bukan dengan mengorbankannya begitu saja.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The interests of society should matter more in some cases, but I do not think they should automatically take priority over individual rights. From my perspective as someone from China, I understand why people emphasize social stability, public order, and collective responsibility, because those can genuinely protect millions of people from chaos and harm. But I also think that if individual rights are treated as secondary all the time, it becomes too easy for authorities or majorities to justify unfairness in the name of the common good. A healthy society should balance both, not sacrifice the individual whenever society claims a higher purpose.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus otomatis didahulukan di atas hak individu. Dari pengalaman melihat Indonesia, alasan “demi kepentingan umum” sering dipakai untuk membenarkan pembatasan yang sebenarnya berlebihan, padahal hak individu justru penting sebagai pagar agar mayoritas atau penguasa tidak semena-mena. Stabilitas dan ketertiban memang perlu, tetapi kalau hak dasar terus dikorbankan, yang lahir bukan masyarakat kuat melainkan ketakutan dan ketidakadilan. Menurut saya, kepentingan sosial harus dicapai lewat pembatasan yang sangat terbatas dan jelas, bukan dengan menempatkan individu selalu di posisi kalah.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not accept the idea that society should automatically come before the individual, even though I understand why that view is common in China. In my experience, collective goals like stability, public order, and economic development can be very important, but when they become a blanket excuse to override rights, it is easy for unfairness to be normalized and for people to lose the confidence to speak up. A society is not truly strong if it protects the whole by making the individual disposable. The better principle is that society can limit rights only in narrow, clearly justified cases, not as a default rule.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak pernyataan itu: kepentingan masyarakat tidak boleh otomatis diprioritaskan di atas hak individu. Bagi saya sebagai orang Indonesia, alasan “demi kepentingan umum” terlalu sering dipakai untuk membenarkan pembatasan yang kebablasan, dan itu berbahaya karena mayoritas atau penguasa bisa merasa selalu benar. Memang ada keadaan tertentu, seperti bencana atau ancaman nyata, ketika pembatasan hak bisa dibenarkan, tetapi harus terbatas, jelas, dan bisa dipertanggungjawabkan. Kalau hak individu dianggap nomor dua terus-menerus, yang lahir bukan masyarakat yang kuat, melainkan budaya takut dan ketidakadilan.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The interests of society should not automatically take priority over the rights of the individual, even though I understand why many people in China value collective stability and order. From my perspective, social goals like development and public safety are important, but once “the common good” becomes a default excuse to override rights, it becomes too easy for unfairness to be justified and for ordinary people to lose any real protection. A strong society is not one that treats individuals as disposable for the sake of harmony. Society can limit rights in exceptional, clearly defined situations, but not as a general principle.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
